candidate_summary: Skipari leader-LLM holds canonical plan; K chorus-LLMs in ring emit refrain_i; refrains aggregated to consensus; step-locked skipari emits next verse only after consensus; entropy-gated paraphrase re-emission on defect.

results.title: Multi-Agent Step-Locked Iterative Consensus
results.reason: Direct match: step-locked iterative consensus is the core RINGDANS mechanism; sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7 AND kw>=2.

decision_rationale: All 8 prior-art results hit on keyword overlap (kw>=2); result #8 (Step-Locked Iterative Consensus) additionally hits on both semantic (0.72) and functional (0.78) thresholds, providing a near-direct mechanism match. Results #1 (leader-follower consensus) and #8 (step-locked consensus) collectively cover the skipari-leader + step-locked-consensus core; orchestration papers (#4,#5) cover centralized direction; retry/cyclic papers (#6,#7) cover entropy-gated re-emission. The RINGDANS-CASCADE mechanism is substantially anticipated by existing multi-agent LLM consensus and orchestration literature, with the cultural framing (ringdans/kvæði) being decorative rather than novel. Verdict: FAIL — not sufficiently novel.
